full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Paper selection and conflict resolution for Part A
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Find a classical ML paper (SVM, GMM, or Time Series) that is light on math, easy to reproduce on toy data, and CORE A* (2009-2012). Ensure it is not already taken by my batchmates in the live sheet.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Guided me away from duplicate SVM/GMM papers and helped me select a perfect Time Series paper (Mueen KDD 2009) that satisfies every single Part A rule and is easy to explain.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I verified the CORE A* venue (KDD 2009), year 2009, methodological Time Series contribution, and CPU-only reproducibility myself by checking the paper PDF and the live response sheet.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Find a classical ML paper (SVM, GMM, or Time Series) that is light on math, easy to reproduce on toy data, and CORE A* (2009-2012). Ensure it is not already taken by my batchmates in the live sheet.
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the critical prompt that helped me navigate away from taken SVM/GMM papers and led me to the exact Time Series Motif paper that perfectly fits the exam constraints and my need for clear reproducibility.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A.
